_doc: Locale registry for the site chrome. Adding a language = add it to `enabled` and translate the keys in data/chrome.json; the guard (scripts/i18n-check.mjs) tells you when you are done. Routing (href/lang/hreflang/canonical) is NEVER listed here or in the catalog — it is derived from route+locale by the generator.
_fallback_doc: `fallback` = strings allowed to stay in the default locale. This is the ONLY escape hatch that silences the guard, so it is governed:
IRON RULE — 'not translated yet' is NEVER a valid reason. Untranslated strings MUST stay red in the guard; that red light is the entire point.
Only things that ESSENTIALLY should not be translated belong here: brand names, product model names, spec tokens, code/URL/e-mail values, proper nouns.
Every entry carries a `reason`. The guard reports the entry count so growth is visible.

NOT the same as a homograph: a word whose correct pt translation happens to equal the English (e.g. 'Industrial', 'FAQ') is a REAL translation — it belongs in data/chrome.json with an explicit pt-BR value and reason:'homograph', NOT here.
_model_display_doc: Single source of truth for model display names (总工 2026-07-14: unify on the SPACED form). The hyphenated form that render.js CATMAP used ('Standard-Actuated') was an artifact of capitalising the URL slug — a trace, not a design. The spaced form is already what the homepage grid, meta_title and the pt h1 wordlist use, and matches Starlink's own naming. Structural constant — NOT translated (pt keeps 'Performance (Gen 3)' verbatim), hence every value also appears in `fallback` above. Consumers: chrome partial, meta_title derivation (R2), CAT_H1, render.js CATMAP (R2, ships as its own user-visible commit).
